checked_queries: missing async jobs fail registry reads
job not found executor is zero continue
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: read_jobs_for_settlement does not return RegistryError when jobExecutor[job_id] is zero; it logs and continues, returning partial/empty results. Only read_jobs_for_spc_verification fails closed.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-async-registry/src/lib.rs read_jobs_for_spc_verification returns RegistryError::DatabaseError for zero executor.
crates/ritual-async-registry/src/lib.rs read_jobs_for_settlement explicitly continues on zero executor.
Payload and verifier callers handle absent settlement jobs after the registry reader omits them.

notes: The invariant requires both settlement and SPC reads to fail; the current port intentionally diverges for settlement reads.
rationale: Both reader functions replace continue on zero executor with a DatabaseError explaining that non-existent jobs cannot be settled or SPC-verified. The commit message states silent fallback masked upstream bugs and should surface immediately. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: read_jobs_for_settlement and read_jobs_for_spc_verification must return RegistryError when AsyncJobTracker.jobExecutor[job_id] is zero, rather than silently skipping that job and returning a partial result.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call each reader with a job ID whose jobExecutor storage slot is zero and assert the function returns RegistryError rather than omitting the job from the returned vector; call with multiple IDs and one missing job to assert no partial success is returned.
summary: Settlement registry reads skip missing jobs instead of failing closed.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call each reader with a job ID whose jobExecutor storage slot is zero and assert the function returns RegistryError rather than omitting the job from the returned vector; call with multiple IDs and one missing job to assert no partial success is returned.
title: Missing async jobs fail registry reads
